title: populus × canadensis var. gelrica
intro: Populus × canadensis var. gelrica, also known as the Black Cottonwood Willow, is a deciduous tree hybrid that grows in the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The bark and leaves of the Black Cottonwood Willow have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of salicylic acid and flavonoids. The health benefits of Populus × canadensis var. gelrica include relief from menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations. The herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, salves, and infusions, and are often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
